Cv.short_about_me: I’m Mikaela, a front-end developer with a background in customer support and a long-time love for creating anything I could shape, design, or bring to life digitally.

text_about_me.1: I’m Mikaela, a front-end development student with a background in customer support and a long-time love for creating things I can shape, design, and bring to life digitally.
text_about_me.1.1: I enjoy translating ideas into clean, thoughtful interfaces and paying attention to the small details that make a user experience feel intuitive.
text_about_me.2: My love for design started early — long before I knew what “front-end” even meant. As a teenager, I spent hours customizing blogs, experimenting with HTML, CSS, and Photoshop.
text_about_me.2.1: It was curiosity at its purest, and the feeling of seeing something I built come to life on screen never really left.
text_about_me.3: I started working at 16, mostly in customer-focused roles, which taught me how to communicate, solve problems, and understand people.
text_about_me.3.1: That experience shaped how I approach development today — thinking not only about how something works, but how it feels to use.
text_about_me.4: Now I’m studying Front End Development, building projects, learning new tools, and improving a little every day.
text_about_me.4.1: I know there’s still a lot for me to learn, and that’s part of what excites me most about this field.
text_about_me.5: Five words to describe me: open, loyal, responsible, inventive & adaptable.
text_about_me.6: I love the energy of collaboration, but I’m just as comfortable working independently — and I’m excited to see where this path takes me next.

description.1: Develop reusable UI components for an expenses management application. Implement features and improvements based on Jira tickets. Contribute to UI and UX improvements to enhance the overall user experience.

Developed an interactive data visualization dashboard using React and TypeScript. Responsible for component structure, state management, and UI design, working independently while collaborating with the team to ensure quality in a production environment.
Studying Front End Development, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Frameworks, Graphic tools, UX, a11y, API development and collaborative group projects.
Handling incoming calls and emails, helping customers with issues, and completing varied administrative tasks. Developed strong communication, problem-solving, and customer-focused service skills.
Assisted customers through email and chat in a fast-paced iGaming environment. Improved written communication, efficiency, and gained insight into the iGaming industry.
Provided daily support to an 11-year-old girl with a disability. Built skills in non-verbal communication, patience, and creative problem-solving.
Handled customer inquiries across multiple channels. Strengthened multitasking abilities, language skills, and service handling under pressure.
Packaged and shipped secure ID documents with high accuracy. Gained independence, reliability, and attention to detail.
This project was developed as a school project at Medieinstitutet.
description.2: We were given a pre-made design to replicate using HTML and CSS for the first time.

description.1: This project was developed as a school project at Medieinstitutet.
description.2: The aim was to create a webshop for donuts or another product of choice. I chose to create a webshop for Canvases I've created in photoshop a few years ago to make it a little bit more personal.
description.3: Throughout this project I focused on understanding the basics of Java Script and I'm excited to redo this project in a few years. This is my first project so I consider it to be the most important one. I hope you enjoy.

description.1: This project was developed as a school group-project at Medieinstitutet.
description.2: The aim was to create a quiz in theme of choice. We chose to create a quiz about cartoons because it felt fun to play with happy colors.
description.3: Throughout this project we focused on learning how to work as a group, both individually and pair coding. I hope you enjoy.

description.1: Welcome to a Design-Swap, an assignment we did while studying the Front End Developer Program at Mediainstitutet.
description.2: In this project we got handed a design from another group and coded the website according to their vision.

description.1: Welcome to the other project of the Design-Swap, an assignment we did while studying the Front End Developer Program at Mediainstitutet.
description.2: In this project we handed over this design and another group code the website according to our vision.

description.1: In this project we built a Book-api with MongoDB as backend and a Frontend interface with Vue. It allows us to browse, review and manage a collection of books.
description.2: The application also includes user authentication, admin-only access for managing content, and public access for viewing books submitting reviews.

description.1: In this project, I built a client-side Todo Application using React and TypeScript, utilizing TailwindCSS to create a responsive and user-friendly interface.
description.2: It allows users to organize their daily tasks, categorize them by emotion, filter active and completed items, and manage their workflow with ease.
description.3: The application also includes state management for tracking task status and sorting preferences, using localStorage for persistence to ensure the user's list remains consistent across page reloads.

description.1: In this project, I built a client-side Zoo Application using React and TypeScript.
description.2: It allows users to browse a collection of animals, view detailed information, and manage their feeding schedules.
description.3: The application also includes time-based state logic for tracking hunger levels and uses localStorage for persistence, ensuring that feeding status remains consistent across page reloads.

description.1: In this project, I built a client-side Job Search Application using React and TypeScript, integrating Arbetsförmedlingen's official design system to create an improved user experience.
description.2: It allows users to search for job advertisements, filter results by location and occupation, view detailed job descriptions, and save interesting positions for later.
description.3: The application also includes state management for tracking search history and saved jobs, using localStorage for persistence to ensure user preferences remain consistent across page reloads.

description.1: In this project, developed during my internship, I built a Privacy Dashboard Application using React and TypeScript to visualize customer health and product usage for the company's Privacy product.
description.2: It provides a robust interface for administrators to view and analyze aggregated statistics, manage customer and user profiles, and track engagement through a custom-built scoring system.
description.3: The application features secure authentication (Clerk), internationalization (i18n), and efficient state management with TanStack Query, initially prototyped with MSW before integrating with a real backend.

description.1: In this project, developed as my final-year examination work at Medieinstitutet, I built Kronspar—a savings web application using React and TypeScript that helps students set aside money over time for their graduation hat without last-minute financial stress.
description.2: It works as a personal finance dashboard: users see current savings, time remaining, and progress toward the goal; configure a saving plan and payment methods; explore statistics over time; compare graduation hat suppliers; and review projected balances based on their monthly contribution.
description.3: The application features secure authentication with Clerk, TanStack Query for data fetching, and React Router for navigation. MSW was used during development to simulate backend interactions ahead of future API integration. A demonstration-only Time Travel control, hidden in the header, jumps between milestones such as hat purchase day and graduation to validate savings projection logic.

description.1: A bilingual marketing site for Amperium Elektriska, an electrician company in Stockholm. I designed and built the site solo from scratch with React, TypeScript and Vite — with a focus on accessibility, i18n and SEO.

what_it_is.heading: What it is
what_it_is.body: A bilingual (Swedish/English) marketing site for Amperium Elektriska, an electrician company in Stockholm founded in 2023. The site presents the company's three services (service & emergency call-outs, electrical installation, project management), the team, and includes a quote request form for new customers. I designed and built the site from scratch, solo, with React, TypeScript and Vite.

my_role.heading: My role
my_role.body: I owned the whole project: structure, design implementation, accessibility, i18n, SEO and testing. I started from my own design system (tokens for color, spacing, typography) and adapted the values to the brand's identity.

items.1: Page structure: Four pages (Home, About, Services, Contact) via React Router, with a shared Layout component (header/nav/footer) as the app shell.
items.2: Component organization: A clear rule for where components belong — page-specific components in pages/<Page>/components/, generic UI primitives in components/ui/ (Button, Input, Pill, etc.), and shared but non-generic components (e.g. ServiceCard) at the top level. That split made it easy to find the right place for new code without guessing.
items.3: Styling: CSS Modules throughout, no CSS-in-JS. Design tokens as CSS variables in styles/tokens/, with a separate layer of semantic tokens (--color-action-primary instead of --color-orange-500) so components never point directly at primitives — making it possible to swap theme/brand color without touching component code.
items.4: Responsiveness: Mobile-first with @custom-media breakpoints (@media (--md)) via PostCSS.
items.5: Swedish routing: Slugs for the service pages are in Swedish (not English) — a deliberate SEO decision to match how Swedish customers actually search.

i18n.heading: Internationalization (i18n)
i18n.body: All text lives in two structurally identical JSON files (sv/en) via react-i18next, with nested keys per page/section. A language switcher sits in the navigation, with automatic browser-language detection and sync against <html lang>.

items.1: Skip link to main content for keyboard and screen reader users.
items.2: Contrast was actively adjusted during the project — e.g. swapping a white background for black/orange, and a darker gray for background-elevated, specifically to improve contrast (documented in my project journal as a deliberate a11y decision, not just aesthetics).
items.3: Focus styles and tested keyboard navigation in the navigation component.

items.1: Structured data (JSON-LD, schema.org/Electrician) with address, opening hours, phone number and service areas — so Google can show company info directly in search results.
items.2: Open Graph metadata, descriptions, and a single source of truth for phone number/email (constants/contact.ts) reused in the footer, nav and contact page — with clear documentation (CONTENT_GUIDE.md) noting that opening hours and phone number exist in two places (code + JSON-LD) that must be kept in sync manually.

testing.heading: Testing
testing.body: Vitest + Testing Library, with tests co-located next to every component that has logic or meaningful markup (navigation, footer, forms, hero, contact card, and more).

process.heading: Process
process.body: I started the process by reviewing the company's existing site and analyzing what to keep stylistically and what needed an upgrade. Based on that analysis, I designed the new look in Figma and talked through a few design decisions together with Claude. I then set up the project structure with a dev branch and a main branch to keep a professional workflow, creating smaller feature branches off dev for each new feature. I built all the reusable components first — starting with navigation and routing, since they form the app's shell — before moving on to page-specific content. I also kept a day-by-day project journal through the whole build, covering everything from that initial setup to forms and SEO polish toward the end.

maintainability.heading: Maintainability
maintainability.body: Wrote a separate CONTENT_GUIDE.md aimed at non-developers (e.g. the company owner) with exact steps for updating phone number, opening hours, contacts and text — without touching the code structure. That was a deliberate choice to make the site maintainable after delivery, without me needing to be involved in every small content change.
